title: ferray-core: no reduction methods (sum, prod, min, max, mean) on Array
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's ndarray has .sum(), .prod(), .min(), .max(), .mean(), .std(), .var(), .any(), .all() as methods, all supporting axis= and keepdims= parameters. These live in numpy._core/_methods.py and fromnumeric.py. ferray-core has fold_axis() as a generic building block but no ergonomic reduction methods. Users must write arr.fold_axis(Axis(0), 0.0, |acc, x| acc + x) instead of arr.sum(Axis(0)). At minimum, sum/prod/min/max/mean should be methods on Array and ArrayView, with optional axis parameter. These are among the most commonly called NumPy functions.
